title: Phase 1: milestone planning and bead-driven execution in Ralph Burning
description:
Context:
Ralph already has a strong execution engine, a staged requirements system, durable project/run state,
and automation/worktree infrastructure. What it lacks is a larger planning-and-routing layer that can
reason about a whole body of work, decompose it into explicit beads, and then drive existing Ralph
execution one bead at a time.

Goal:
Adapt Ralph to a flywheel-style workflow while staying close to the current system. The milestone is
the new umbrella unit. One current Ralph project/run becomes the execution container for one bead
(user-facing term: task). The canonical graph lives in `.beads/` and is managed through br/bv.

Phase-1 intent:
- add a milestone layer above the current project/run substrate
- extend full requirements into a milestone planner
- export milestone work into beads/epics in `.beads/`
- select the next bead with bv/br
- execute each bead through a milestone-aware quick_dev loop
- reconcile review outcomes back into `.beads/`
- keep execution sequential for the first release

Guardrails:
- do not build a duplicate internal task graph for phase 1
- do not replace the quick_dev workflow shape
- do not make sprints the default execution abstraction
- do not force a global internal rename from project -> task before behavior is proven
- do not attempt parallel bead execution in the first release

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the end-to-end path exists from idea to milestone plan, bead export, next-bead selection, task execution, milestone-aware review, and bead reconciliation
- `.beads/` remains the canonical work graph while the existing Ralph project-run substrate stays in use for one active bead task at a time
- phase 1 remains sequential and deliberately stops short of parallel bead execution

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Why this epic exists:
The point of this effort is not to bolt a new orchestration system beside Ralph. It is to give Ralph
a larger planning horizon while preserving the parts that already work: current requirements stages,
the quick_dev execution loop, durable run history, and the existing runtime/worktree substrate.

What future us should remember:
1. The milestone layer is the new abstraction; the current project/run is still the execution substrate.
2. br/bv are the source of truth for bead-space in phase 1.
3. Review must be whole-plan-aware so it does not keep asking for work that already belongs to later beads.
4. New bead creation must be intentionally conservative.

title: Define phase-1 vocabulary, invariants, and acceptance boundaries
description:
Purpose:
Capture the shared language and architectural cut lines for the integration before implementation fans out.

This epic exists because the current Ralph codebase still uses `project` as its main durable execution unit,
while the new user-facing model introduces `milestone` as the umbrella body of work and `task` as the
execution of a single bead. Without explicit rules, teams will mix the terms and accidentally design the
wrong abstractions.

Deliverables:
- glossary for milestone / task / bead / epic / project
- phase-1 invariants and non-goals
- map of current Ralph modules to future responsibilities
- release acceptance checklist for the integrated behavior

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- canonical vocabulary, guardrails, module mapping, and the cutover checklist are all captured for the phase-1 rollout
- implementation beads can reference this epic for naming, scope, and acceptance boundaries instead of inventing local rules

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
This epic is intentionally meta and front-loaded. The goal is to prevent architectural drift while several
implementation tracks proceed in parallel.

title: Publish the glossary and naming rules for milestone, task, bead, epic, and project
description:
Goal:
Write the canonical terminology for the new system.

Must define:
- milestone = umbrella unit containing one plan and many beads
- bead = canonical work item in `.beads/`
- task = one Ralph-managed execution of one bead
- epic = grouped bead in `.beads/`
- project = legacy/internal execution substrate retained during transition

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- user-facing docs and CLI language prefer milestone/task terminology
- internal compatibility rule explains when `project` still appears
- docs make clear that one current Ralph project ~= one task execution container

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Naming is not cosmetic here. If we fail to separate milestone from task, we will either overgrow the old
project abstraction or accidentally create a second execution substrate.

title: Write the phase-1 architecture guardrails and explicit non-goals
description:
Goal:
Record the rules that keep the first implementation small, coherent, and compatible with current Ralph.

Must include:
- use br/bv directly; do not build a duplicate internal task graph in phase 1
- reuse quick_dev shape instead of inventing a new bead workflow
- keep execution sequential initially
- treat review as milestone-aware, not sprint-oriented
- create new beads only when fix-now and planned-elsewhere are both wrong
- keep current project/run substrate internally until the new naming is proven

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- a short design note exists and is referenced by major implementation epics
- guardrails are reflected in milestone planner, review logic, and controller code

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
This issue is the brake pedal. When implementation gets exciting, this is the issue that reminds us what
*not* to build yet.

title: Map current Ralph modules to their milestone-era responsibilities
description:
Goal:
Produce a code-aware responsibility map so implementation tasks stay grounded in the current repo.

Expected mappings:
- `requirements_drafting` -> milestone planner
- `project_run_record` -> task execution substrate with milestone/bead linkage
- `workflow_composition` -> milestone-aware quick_dev execution/review
- `automation_runtime` -> future home for parallel milestone dispatch, but not replaced in phase 1
- new `milestone_*` context -> milestone storage/state/orchestration
- `cli/*` -> milestone/task command surface and compatibility shims

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the map points at concrete directories/files under `src/contexts/*`, `src/cli/*`, and docs
- every major implementation epic references the owning modules

comments.author: planner
comments.text: This is the issue that keeps the plan Ralph-native instead of drifting into an abstract greenfield design.

title: Write the phase-1 acceptance criteria and cutover checklist
description:
Goal:
Turn the integration goal into a concrete checklist that can drive conformance, manual smoke, and release decisions.

Must cover:
- milestone planning success
- bead graph creation and sync behavior
- next bead selection
- task creation from bead context
- milestone-aware review classifications
- conservative new bead creation
- successful bead close and advancement to the next bead

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- checklist is specific enough to drive tests and signoff
- checklist clearly distinguishes phase-1 done from later parallelism work

title: Conformance, integration tests, docs, and operator hardening for phase 1
description:
Purpose:
Turn the new design into something operable and releasable.

This epic covers:
- unit and integration tests
- conformance scenarios
- operator docs for milestone + br workflows
- explicit documentation of deferred parallelism

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- unit, integration, conformance, and operator-facing documentation cover the phase-1 milestone system at the right depth
- deferred parallelism is documented as future work without pulling implementation into phase 1

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
The controller, planner, and review changes cross many modules. This epic exists so the final system is not only
implemented, but also testable, operable, and explainable.

title: Add unit tests for milestone models, planners, prompt generation, and adapters
description:
Goal:
Cover the new model/service boundaries with focused tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone model/storage/service tests exist
- planner contract/rendering tests exist
- br/bv adapter tests exist
- prompt generation tests exist

title: Add an end-to-end scenario: idea -> milestone plan -> bead graph -> task run -> bead close
description:
Goal:
Prove the full integrated happy path once, end to end.

Scope boundaries:
- this scenario is the successful bead-close path from idea through milestone execution
- it should not wait on the separate new-bead-creation policy to land before the base system can be exercised

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the scenario covers milestone planning, bead export, next-bead selection, task creation, quick_dev execution, successful reconciliation, and bead close
- the scenario asserts that the happy path does not create unexpected new beads
- the scenario is automated and deterministic enough to rerun during development

comments.author: planner
comments.text: This is the closest thing to a reality check for phase 1. If this scenario is weak, the architecture probably is too.

title: Assemble the scenario-specific temp workspace with .beads and milestone artifacts for integration testing
description:
Goal:
Use the shared QA6 builders/helpers to create one reproducible fixture repo/workspace for the end-to-end milestone scenario.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
1. The fixture includes milestone artifacts and a beads workspace.
2. Tests can run without depending on a developer's real repo state.
3. Scenario-specific setup reuses the shared test infrastructure from QA6.

title: Script the end-to-end acceptance scenario and assertions
description:
Goal:
Turn the fixture into a meaningful automated happy-path scenario.

Assertions should verify:
- milestone artifacts are created and populated
- the selected bead is claimed and linked to a Ralph task
- the active task completes and reconciles through the normal success path
- milestone status and bead state advance without spawning surprise work

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- assertions cover milestone creation, bead export, selection, task creation, task completion, successful reconciliation, and status updates
- the scenario verifies the active bead is closed and the next-step signal is recorded after success
- the scenario verifies that no unexpected new bead is created on the happy path
- failures surface enough log and state context to diagnose which phase broke

comments.author: planner
comments.text: Implementation requirement: the e2e scenario script must capture structured log output from the system under test at each phase. On assertion failure, the script must dump: (1) the structured log lines from the failing phase, (2) the filesystem state of .ralph-burning/ and .beads/ at that point, and (3) a clear description of what was expected vs what happened. The script should produce a human-readable summary suitable for debugging by an operator who did not write the test.

comments.author: codex
comments.text: Boundary note: this bead owns the scenario steps and domain assertions for the end-to-end acceptance flow. The reusable runner/reporting/log-preservation harness lives in ralph-burning-9ni.10.7 so scenario logic and execution/reporting infrastructure can evolve independently.

title: Add conformance coverage for milestone-aware quick_dev review behavior
description:
Goal:
Lock in the whole-plan-aware review behavior so it does not regress.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- conformance scenarios cover fix-now, planned-elsewhere, and propose-new-bead outcomes
- approval behavior is tested for a bead whose later work already exists in the graph

title: Document operator workflow for br sync, milestone runs, recovery, and review outcomes
description:
Goal:
Write the runbook future operators will actually need.

Must cover:
- normal milestone planning/export/run flow
- br sync expectations
- recovery after git pull / sync conflicts / failed task run
- meaning of review classifications
- when a new bead is expected vs suspicious

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria: - docs are specific enough to unblock an operator who did not build the system

title: Capture the deferred phase-2 parallel execution design on top of existing worktree leases
description:
Goal:
Preserve the shape of later parallel milestone execution without pulling it into phase 1.

Must cover:
- one worktree lease per active bead task
- controller concurrency limits
- interaction with br claims and bead readiness
- how bv might help with parallel track selection later

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- a compact design note exists for the deferred parallel direction
- the note explains how phase-1 sequential code can evolve without breaking the worktree-lease model
- implementation remains explicitly out of scope for this bead

comments.author: planner
comments.text: This is intentionally a follow-on design capture, not part of the phase-1 critical path.

title: Build shared test infrastructure: mock br/bv adapters, fixture builders, and log capture helpers
description:
Goal:
Create a reusable test infrastructure module that all test beads can depend on, eliminating
duplicated setup code and providing consistent test patterns across the milestone system.

Components:

1. Mock br adapter:
   - Implements the same typed interface as the real br adapter
   - Returns configurable responses for create, update, close, dep add, sync, etc.
   - Tracks all calls for assertion (verify what mutations were requested)
   - Can simulate errors (command not found, malformed output, sync failure)
   - Configurable latency for timeout testing

2. Mock bv adapter:
   - Implements the same typed interface as the real bv adapter
   - Returns configurable next-bead recommendations, triage summaries
   - Can simulate empty graph, blocked graph, single ready bead, multiple ready beads
   - Tracks all calls for assertion

3. Milestone fixture builders:
   - Builder-pattern API for creating realistic milestone state in temp directories
   - Supports pre-populating journal events, task linkage, plan artifacts
   - Bead graph fixtures for creating .beads/ state with configurable epics, tasks, deps

4. Log capture helpers:
   - Capture tracing output during test execution
   - Assert on structured fields (e.g., assert that milestone_id and operation appear in logs)
   - Capture and display logs on test failure for debugging
   - Compatible with both unit tests and integration tests

5. Temp workspace helper:
   - Creates a complete temp workspace with .ralph-burning/ and .beads/ directories
   - Configurable initial state (empty, with milestones, with beads, etc.)
   - Automatic cleanup on drop
   - Provides paths for all expected directories

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- Mock adapters exist for br and bv operations with full call tracking
- Fixture builders can create realistic milestone/bead state in one call
- Log capture can assert on specific structured fields
- Temp workspace helper creates valid directory structures
- All helpers are documented with usage examples in doc comments
- Test infrastructure is in a dedicated module (e.g., tests/support/ or src/test_support/)
  accessible from all test files

title: Add e2e test runner with step-by-step logging, timing, and human-readable result reports
description:
Goal:
Wrap the e2e acceptance scenario in a test runner that captures detailed execution traces,
produces human-readable reports, and integrates with CI pipelines.

Requirements:
- each major phase is reported as a named step with timing and pass/fail state
- failure output includes the relevant structured logs and filesystem snapshot
- the runner emits both human-readable text and machine-readable JSON
- the runner reuses QA6 helpers and the QA10.2.2 scenario instead of creating a second scenario definition

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
1. Running the e2e suite produces a clearly formatted step-by-step report.
2. Each step shows pass-fail with timing.
3. Failed steps include relevant log output and filesystem state.
4. The report is readable by an operator unfamiliar with the system.
5. CI can interpret the result through exit code and JSON summary.
6. The runner can complete a full pass in under 2 minutes on a standard machine.

title: Backlog: parallel milestone execution on top of existing worktree leases
description:
Purpose:
Track the explicitly deferred work needed to run multiple ready beads in parallel later.

This epic is here so future-us does not forget the intended direction, but it should stay out of the phase-1
execution path.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- deferred design work names the constraints for safe multi-bead execution on top of existing worktree leases
- these backlog items stay out of the phase-1 execution path until the sequential controller is proven

comments.author: planner
comments.text: Keep this epic deferred until sequential milestone execution is proven.

title: Design controller concurrency around one worktree lease per active bead task
description:
Goal:
Define how the milestone controller will scale to multiple active bead tasks without shared-worktree chaos.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- design fits the current automation_runtime lease model
- ownership and cleanup semantics are explicit

title: Design multi-bead selection and capacity rules using bv guidance
description:
Goal:
Define how future parallel execution chooses multiple independent beads safely.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- design explains how bv planning/triage/capacity outputs inform parallel choice
- deferred until sequential controller behavior is stable

title: Add milestone domain model, storage, and status tracking
description:
Purpose:
Introduce the new umbrella unit above the existing project/run substrate.

The milestone is the durable holder of:
- the original request
- the refined plan
- milestone-level status
- bead/task lineage
- milestone journal/history

This epic should create the storage/service/query layer that lets the rest of the system talk about a
larger body of work without breaking the current project-run model.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone state, status, lifecycle, and bead-task linkage can be created, updated, and inspected durably under `.ralph-burning/milestones`
- milestone storage remains manually inspectable and safe enough for operator recovery and later controller use

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Phase-1 note:
Keep the milestone store beside existing `.ralph-burning/projects/` data. Do not try to migrate the
project substrate out from under the current system.

title: Define the milestone record schema and filesystem layout under .ralph-burning/milestones
description:
Goal:
Define the canonical on-disk shape for milestone state.

Expected artifacts:
- `milestone.toml`
- `plan.md`
- `plan.json`
- `status.json`
- `journal.ndjson`
- `task-runs/` or equivalent lineage mapping

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- schema is versioned and explicit about milestone identity, status, plan hash/version, active bead, and progress
- storage sits cleanly beside existing project storage
- layout is simple enough to inspect and debug manually

title: Implement milestone store and service layer for create/load/update/journal operations
description:
Goal:
Build the code path that persists and mutates milestone state.

Scope:
- load/save milestone records
- append milestone journal events
- read/write plan artifacts
- maintain task/bead linkage records
- keep writes atomic enough to match existing Ralph durability expectations

Likely target areas:
- new `src/contexts/milestone_*`
- adapters/fs integration
- CLI-facing service methods

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone state can be created and updated without touching `.beads/` yet
- service layer exposes the minimum operations needed by planner, controller, and CLI

comments.author: planner
comments.text: Keep this domain boring and durable. The controller and planner should depend on it, not reimplement it.

title: Add milestone status aggregation and progress queries
description:
Goal:
Compute milestone progress in a way that is useful for both CLI output and controller logic.

Must answer:
- how many beads exist
- how many are open/in progress/done/blocked
- which bead is active
- which Ralph task/run currently owns that bead
- whether the milestone is complete, blocked, or awaiting operator input

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- query surfaces exist for `milestone show` / `milestone status`
- progress is derived from milestone state plus `.beads/` reads, not chat memory

title: Track bead-to-task run linkage inside milestone state
description:
Goal:
Record which Ralph task/project/run attempted which bead, with enough detail to audit retries and outcomes later.

Must capture:
- milestone_id
- bead_id
- current task/project id
- run id if available
- outcome summary
- prompt version or plan hash if relevant

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone state can answer 'what happened to bead X?' without reconstructing from unrelated artifacts
- controller can update linkage after each run outcome

title: Add milestone lifecycle transitions and journal events
description:
Goal:
Define milestone lifecycle states and the events that move between them.

Suggested states:
- planning
- ready
- running
- blocked
- completed
- failed / needs_operator

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- state transitions are explicit and validated
- journal events provide enough detail for debugging and operator recovery
- controller logic can rely on these states instead of inferring status indirectly

title: Add structured tracing spans and log fields for milestone, controller, and bead operations
description:
Goal:
Instrument all milestone-facing code paths with structured tracing so operators can debug
and monitor the system effectively.

Must cover:
- Milestone CRUD operations (create, load, update, delete)
- Lifecycle state transitions (planning → ready → running → completed, etc.)
- Journal event appends and reads
- Controller operations (bead selection, claim, execution start/stop, reconciliation)
- br/bv adapter command invocations (command, arguments, duration, exit code)
- Bead graph mutations (create, close, dependency add, sync)

Structured fields (used consistently across all spans):
- milestone_id
- bead_id
- task_id (project/run id)
- operation (human-readable operation name)
- stage (for workflow operations)

Requirements:
- Use the tracing crate with structured fields (tracing::info_span!, tracing::instrument)
- Follow existing Ralph tracing patterns from other contexts
- All error paths must log the full error chain with context
- Performance-sensitive paths should include timing spans
- Log levels should be meaningful: ERROR for failures, WARN for degraded, INFO for operations, DEBUG for internals

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- All milestone-facing code paths emit structured traces
- Log output can be filtered by milestone_id, bead_id, or task_id
- Error paths include enough context for debugging without source code access
- A RUST_LOG=ralph_burning::contexts::milestone=debug style filter works correctly
- Controller loop iterations produce a clear trace showing what was decided and why

title: Add integration tests for milestone store CRUD, journal, lifecycle, and linkage
description:
Goal:
Prove the milestone store implementation with comprehensive integration tests that exercise
the full API surface on real filesystem state.

Test categories:

1. Milestone CRUD:
   - Create a new milestone and verify on-disk artifacts (milestone.toml, status.json, journal.ndjson)
   - Load an existing milestone and verify all fields round-trip correctly
   - Update milestone fields and verify persistence
   - List milestones in a workspace
   - Handle missing/corrupted milestone directories gracefully

2. Journal events:
   - Append events and verify NDJSON format
   - Read events in chronological order
   - Verify event schema (timestamp, actor, event type, payload)
   - Handle truncated/corrupted journal files

3. Lifecycle transitions:
   - Test all valid transitions (planning → ready → running → completed, etc.)
   - Test that invalid transitions are rejected with clear errors
   - Verify journal events are emitted for each transition
   - Test transition preconditions (e.g., cannot transition to running without an active bead)

4. Bead-to-task linkage:
   - Record a task run against a specific bead
   - Query which task ran which bead
   - Handle multiple attempts (retry history)
   - Verify linkage survives milestone reload

5. Recovery:
   - Verify atomic writes do not produce partial state
   - Recover from interrupted writes where possible
   - Verify temp directory isolation between tests

All tests must:
- Use temporary directories (no shared filesystem state)
- Produce clear assertion messages on failure
- Use the test infrastructure helpers from QA6 where available
- Run in under 5 seconds total

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- Full CRUD coverage for milestone store
- All lifecycle transitions tested (valid and invalid)
- Journal persistence tested with schema validation
- Bead linkage CRUD tested
- Error conditions produce clear error messages
- Tests are self-contained and parallelizable

title: Extend requirements_drafting into a milestone planner
description:
Purpose:
Turn the current full requirements pipeline into the source of truth for milestone-scale planning.

Instead of ending only in a single project seed, the full pipeline should produce a versioned milestone bundle
containing the umbrella plan, acceptance framing, workstreams, and bead-generation instructions.

This is where the large-context reasoning belongs. The implementation loop should consume the output, not re-do it.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- `requirements_drafting` can emit a MilestoneBundle plus durable plan artifacts suitable for bead export
- existing quick requirements and project-seed flows remain coherent during rollout

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Planner principle:
do the hard reasoning once in plan space while the whole milestone still fits in context. The rest of the system
should operate on the resulting explicit structure.

title: Define a versioned MilestoneBundle contract and renderer set
description:
Goal:
Define the machine-readable and human-readable output of milestone planning.

Bundle should include:
- milestone identity/name
- executive summary
- goals and non-goals
- constraints and assumptions
- acceptance map
- workstreams/epics
- bead proposals and dependency hints
- recommended default flow for bead execution
- generated AGENTS guidance seed material

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- schema/versioning strategy is clear
- renderers can emit deterministic `plan.md` and `plan.json`
- downstream bead export has a stable input contract

title: Extend the full requirements pipeline so it can emit a MilestoneBundle
description:
Goal:
Wire milestone planning into the existing full-mode stage machine.

Scope:
- decide whether milestone output extends `project_seed` or introduces a new final stage
- persist milestone-oriented committed stage output
- preserve current caching, rollback, and atomic stage commit behavior
- ensure question rounds still invalidate only the correct downstream artifacts

Likely target areas:
- `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/model.rs`
- `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/service.rs`
- `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/contracts.rs`
- `src/contexts/requirements_drafting/renderers.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- a full requirements run can end in a milestone bundle
- stage reuse and invalidation semantics remain coherent
- the change does not quietly break existing quick mode behavior

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
This is the beating heart of the integration. If this output is weak or unstable, the rest of the system
will compensate badly and drift back into ad-hoc chat planning.

title: Wire MilestoneBundle through the full-mode stage machine and run state
description:
Goal:
Add the new output type to the requirements run state and committed stage bookkeeping.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- run state can identify milestone-oriented completion
- payload/artifact ids for milestone output are stored consistently
- backward compatibility for older seed-oriented runs is preserved where required

title: Preserve question-round invalidation semantics when milestone outputs change
description:
Goal:
Ensure that answer-driven invalidation still removes the correct downstream stages and nothing more.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- changing answers invalidates milestone outputs that depend on synthesis onward
- ideation/research reuse remains valid where intended
- tests prove this behavior

title: Keep requirements show/status surfaces intelligible with milestone-oriented runs
description:
Goal:
Make sure operators can tell what kind of run they are looking at and what the final output is.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- requirements show indicates milestone-oriented completion clearly
- outputs remain readable for both old and new flows

title: Render plan.md, plan.json, and an explicit acceptance map from the milestone planner
description:
Goal:
Produce durable plan artifacts that humans and downstream code can both consume.

Deliverables:
- `plan.md` for human review
- `plan.json` for machine use
- explicit acceptance map linked to workstreams/beads

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- artifacts are deterministic for the same inputs
- they carry enough structure to drive bead creation and review scope boundaries

title: Generate workstreams, epics, bead specs, and dependency hints from the planning output
description:
Goal:
Make the planner output explicit enough that bead creation is mostly translation, not another free-form planning pass.

Must include:
- milestone root epic proposal
- child epic/workstream proposals
- bead specs with rationale, scope, and acceptance criteria
- dependency hints suitable for br dep edges
- notes about what is intentionally deferred to later beads

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the output is strong enough for a deterministic first export into `.beads/`
- review can later recognize 'planned elsewhere' because the planner named that work explicitly

title: Create a milestone record automatically from completed planner output
description:
Goal:
Once a milestone plan is approved/generated, create the milestone record without manual copy/paste.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- completed planner output can materialize a milestone entry
- milestone state stores references to the plan artifacts and bundle version
- operators do not have to hand-wire milestone metadata after planning

title: Preserve current quick requirements and project-seed bootstrap behavior during rollout
description:
Goal:
Introduce milestone planning without breaking today's single-project flows.

Must preserve:
- quick requirements path
- project create from requirements seed
- project bootstrap behavior
- existing docs and tests where the legacy path remains valid

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the new milestone planner is additive or carefully gated
- legacy project-seed paths still work until explicitly replaced

title: Add requirements tests for cache reuse, invalidation, and milestone bundle handoff
description:
Goal:
Prove that the planner remains safe under the same operational conditions as the current requirements system.

Must test:
- cache reuse
- downstream invalidation
- question round resume
- milestone bundle creation
- legacy compatibility where still supported

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria: - test coverage exists at model/service level and at least one CLI-visible path

title: Add br/bv integration for the canonical bead graph
description:
Purpose:
Make `.beads/` the canonical work graph for the new milestone workflow.

Ralph should not invent a second internal graph in phase 1. Instead it needs reliable adapters around:
- br for create/update/comment/dependencies/claim/close/sync
- bv for next-bead recommendations and graph-aware analysis

This epic owns that integration boundary.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- Ralph talks to the canonical bead graph through typed br and bv adapters rather than scattered shell-outs
- milestone export, sync safety, and adapter tests make `.beads/` trustworthy for planning and execution

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Rule of thumb:
if the state belongs to bead-space, prefer putting it in `.beads/` and talking to it through br/bv instead of
creating a competing Ralph-only representation.

title: Implement a br adapter for create/update/comments/dependencies/claim/close/sync operations
description:
Goal:
Wrap br command execution behind a typed boundary Ralph can trust.

Needed capabilities:
- create epic/task issues
- update fields and labels
- add comments
- add/remove dependencies
- claim and close beads
- sync/export/import status checks
- parse machine-readable outputs where available

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- adapter surfaces typed success/error results
- command failures are translated into actionable app errors
- controller and planner code do not manually shell out all over the codebase

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
The main thing to remember is that br is intentionally non-invasive. Export/import/sync behavior is part of the
functional contract here, not an afterthought.

title: Add process execution wrapper and structured command errors for br
description:
Goal:
Centralize shell execution, exit-code handling, stdout/stderr capture, and error classification for br commands.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- missing binary, command failure, malformed output, and workspace misuse are distinct error classes
- callers do not have to parse raw process failures themselves

title: Parse and normalize br JSON output for ready/show/list/dep operations
description:
Goal:
Convert br output into typed structures Ralph can use for planner/controller decisions.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- ready list, show detail, dependency tree/list, and status surfaces are normalized
- parsing code is isolated and testable

title: Support br mutation flows with audit-friendly logging and explicit sync calls
description:
Goal:
Handle create/update/comment/close/reopen/dependency mutations and follow them with the correct sync discipline.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- mutation helpers can optionally force `br sync --flush-only` at the right moments
- controller and planner can trace what changed and why

title: Implement a bv adapter for next-bead, triage, related-work, and impact analysis
description:
Goal:
Give Ralph a typed way to ask bv for graph-aware recommendations.

Needed capabilities:
- next bead selection
- triage summaries
- related-work lookups
- impact/file-overlap checks where useful
- future support for capacity/parallelization analysis

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- adapter can read the few bv commands phase 1 actually needs
- output is normalized enough for controller and prompt generator use

title: Materialize a MilestoneBundle into a root epic, epics, beads, and dependencies in .beads
description:
Goal:
Translate milestone planning output into actual beads managed by br.

Scope:
- create a milestone root epic
- create child epics/workstreams
- create task beads
- add dependency edges
- attach planning rationale/comments where useful
- sync the resulting `.beads/` state

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- a milestone plan can be exported deterministically into `.beads/`
- the initial graph is rich enough for bv/br routing without hand-editing

title: Codify sync/import safety rules for non-invasive br operation
description:
Goal:
Make sure Ralph handles br's non-invasive model safely.

Must cover:
- when to `sync --flush-only`
- when to `sync --import-only`
- how to react to stale DB / malformed JSONL / conflict markers
- what operator recovery looks like after git pull / merge / failed run

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the rules are encoded in code paths where possible
- docs and recovery guidance exist for the rest

title: Add read/query models for bead details, blocker state, and ready work
description:
Goal:
Give prompt generation, controller logic, and CLI read commands a common view of bead-space.

Should answer:
- bead detail
- parent epic
- dependency/blocker status
- ready list
- related/planned-elsewhere candidate beads

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- callers can ask for read models instead of shelling out ad hoc
- data is rich enough to support milestone-aware prompt construction

title: Add adapter tests and error handling for missing tools, malformed output, and sync failures
description:
Goal:
Make the br/bv integration operationally trustworthy before the controller depends on it.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- missing binary paths are handled cleanly
- malformed JSON / unexpected output is surfaced with actionable context
- sync failure paths are covered in tests

comments.author: planner
comments.text: Scope clarification: this bead must cover BOTH error handling AND happy-path tests. The title emphasizes error handling, but the adapter tests should also verify successful create/update/close/sync flows, correct JSON parsing of well-formed output, and nominal dependency operations. Error-only testing is insufficient.

title: Bridge the current project/run substrate into milestone-scoped task execution
description:
Purpose:
Reuse the current Ralph project/run model as the substrate for one bead execution, while introducing task and
milestone concepts above it.

This epic is where current `project` semantics get linked to:
- milestone_id
- bead_id
- plan/prompt versioning
- bead-backed task creation and lineage queries
- substrate compatibility needed by task CLI wrappers, without owning the wrapper UX itself

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- one bead can be executed as one Ralph task-project substrate with durable milestone and bead linkage
- provenance, history, and compatibility are strong enough for task aliases without renaming the internals wholesale

comments.author: planner
comments.text: This is the compatibility epic. We want the new behavior without forcing a risky global rename first.

title: Define task-mode metadata linking current project records to milestone_id and bead_id
description:
Goal:
Extend the current project/run metadata model so a run can be understood as the implementation of a bead.

Expected metadata:
- milestone_id
- bead_id
- parent epic or root epic when useful
- source = milestone
- task mode / execution mode
- prompt or plan version info where needed

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- lineage can be recovered from durable state
- existing project/run semantics remain valid for non-milestone tasks

title: Add a task creation path that bootstraps a project/run from a bead-backed prompt
description:
Goal:
Create one Ralph execution unit directly from selected bead context.

Scope:
- take milestone + bead input
- generate or accept a bead-backed prompt
- create the current project substrate
- set initial task/milestone metadata
- keep the result compatible with normal `run start`

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- controller can create a task/project for a selected bead without manual setup
- created task is durable and inspectable through existing run machinery

title: Track prompt version, plan hash, and source metadata on bead-backed task runs
description:
Goal:
Persist enough provenance to tell whether a task was run against the latest milestone plan/prompt.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- run/task state stores prompt hash or plan hash
- controller and CLI can detect obvious drift between current milestone plan and historical task context

title: Expose milestone and bead lineage in run history and task detail queries
description:
Goal:
Make it easy to inspect which task/run belongs to which bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- run/task detail can display milestone/bead linkage
- history views are useful enough for debugging retries and review outcomes

title: Add task-facing aliases while keeping the internal project substrate intact
description:
Goal:
Introduce task terminology to users without ripping out existing project code.

Scope:
- add CLI aliases/wrappers where appropriate
- keep internal storage/services on the project substrate in phase 1
- document the mapping clearly in output/help

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- user-facing task commands exist or the pathway to them is explicit
- backward compatibility for current project commands is preserved
close_reason: Merged into CLI alias/compatibility beads ralph-burning-9ni.9.2 and ralph-burning-9ni.9.4

title: Add tests for task-mode metadata, bead-backed task creation, and milestone linkage queries
description:
Goal:
Verify the task substrate modifications with focused tests that prove metadata linking,
bead-backed task creation, and lineage queries work correctly.

Test categories:

1. Task-mode metadata:
   - Create a task-mode project record with milestone_id and bead_id
   - Verify metadata persists across load/save cycles
   - Verify non-milestone tasks remain unaffected (backward compatibility)
   - Test metadata validation (required fields, format)

2. Bead-backed task creation:
   - Create a task from bead context (milestone + bead + prompt)
   - Verify the resulting project record has correct metadata
   - Verify the task is compatible with normal run start
   - Test creation failure paths (missing bead, missing milestone)

3. Lineage queries:
   - Query tasks by milestone_id
   - Query tasks by bead_id
   - Get bead execution history (all tasks that attempted a given bead)
   - Verify queries work correctly with mixed milestone/non-milestone tasks

4. Prompt version tracking:
   - Store prompt hash on task creation
   - Detect drift between current plan and historical task
   - Verify hash is deterministic for identical inputs

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- Task-mode metadata round-trips correctly
- Bead-backed creation produces valid, runnable tasks
- Lineage queries return correct results
- Backward compatibility with existing non-milestone tasks verified
- Tests run in isolation with temp directories

title: Generate milestone-aware prompts and AGENTS guidance for each bead task
description:
Purpose:
Feed the current workflow engine better context instead of replacing it.

Each bead-backed task prompt needs:
- milestone summary
- current bead scope
- dependencies and parent epic
- acceptance criteria
- already-planned-elsewhere context
- repo operating guidance / AGENTS rules

This is the main seam that makes quick_dev whole-plan-aware.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone-aware prompts and AGENTS guidance are generated deterministically for each active bead task
- prompt context includes enough nearby work to prevent scope bleed without dumping the whole graph

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Important constraint:
include enough whole-plan context to prevent false review feedback, but not so much that the active task prompt
becomes bloated and unfocused.

title: Define the task prompt contract for bead execution
description:
Goal:
Define the canonical input shape for a milestone-aware bead execution prompt.

Should include sections for:
- milestone summary
- current bead details
- must-do scope
- explicit non-goals
- acceptance criteria
- already planned elsewhere
- review policy
- AGENTS/repo guidance

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- prompt generator and workflow stages share one clear contract
- contract is stable enough to hash/version for drift detection

title: Generate prompt.md from milestone summary, bead scope, dependencies, and planned-elsewhere context
description:
Goal:
Build the actual task prompt generator for bead-backed execution.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- prompt.md is generated deterministically
- the current bead is clearly scoped
- nearby/future bead references explain what should *not* be absorbed into the active task
- output works with the existing workflow engine's prompt consumption model

title: Generate milestone-scoped AGENTS guidance and define how Ralph updates repo AGENTS.md
description:
Goal:
Produce durable operating guidance that survives long runs and resumptions.

Must cover:
- repo conventions
- milestone priorities
- bead workflow rules
- review policy for fix-now vs planned-elsewhere vs new-bead
- validation expectations

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone planning can generate AGENTS guidance
- Ralph has a clear strategy for inserting/updating a managed section in repo AGENTS.md

title: Inject nearby and future bead context without overwhelming the working prompt
description:
Goal:
Decide how much graph context belongs in the active task prompt and how it should be summarized.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- prompt includes enough neighboring context to explain 'planned elsewhere'
- prompt does not dump the entire milestone graph or swamp the active implementation task
- heuristics are deterministic and testable

title: Add tests for prompt determinism, hashing, and milestone-aware rendering
description:
Goal:
Prove that prompt generation is stable and suitable for drift detection and reproducible runs.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- prompts hash deterministically for identical inputs
- rendered content includes the required milestone/bead sections
- tests cover the planned-elsewhere section and AGENTS inclusion

title: Make quick_dev milestone-aware without replacing its shape
description:
Purpose:
Keep the current quick_dev sequence:
1. plan_and_implement
2. review
3. apply_fixes
4. final_review

But make each stage understand the whole milestone well enough to avoid repeatedly suggesting work that is
already planned for later beads.

This epic owns the behavioral change, not a new workflow shape.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- quick_dev keeps its current stage shape while enforcing bead scope and milestone-aware review routing
- planned-elsewhere and conservative new-bead behavior are explicit and test-covered

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
The core review distinction we need is:
- fix now
- already planned elsewhere
- genuinely missing work

That distinction is what makes bead-by-bead execution viable without collapsing back into 'do the whole project now'.

title: Teach plan_and_implement to honor bead scope, non-goals, and milestone context
description:
Goal:
Make the first stage of quick_dev operate as the implementation of one bead, not the whole milestone.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- stage prompt sees milestone summary, current bead, and out-of-scope notes
- implementation output is shaped by bead acceptance criteria, not global ambition

title: Extend review and final-review contracts with fix-now / planned-elsewhere / new-bead classifications
description:
Goal:
Enrich the structured outputs so review findings can be routed correctly.

Needed classes:
- fix_current_bead
- covered_by_existing_bead
- propose_new_bead
- informational_only

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- schemas/contracts support the classification
- domain validation enforces meaningful payloads
- downstream stages can consume the result without guesswork

comments.author: planner
comments.text: This issue is central because it bridges the old task-local review world and the new milestone-aware routing world.

title: Define the new review classification schema and domain validation rules
description:
Goal:
Put the new classification categories into the structured contracts Ralph validates.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- schemas are explicit
- domain validation prevents nonsensical combinations
- tests cover common and edge cases

title: Render classification guidance into review and final_review prompts
description:
Goal:
Teach the reviewers what the new categories mean and when to use them.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- prompts define the routing choices clearly
- reviewers are told to be conservative about proposing new beads

title: Update the review stage to avoid demanding work already assigned to later beads
description:
Goal:
Use milestone context to keep the review loop scoped to the current bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- review can explicitly mark issues as already planned elsewhere
- such findings do not bounce the current task back into fix loops
- milestone/bead identifiers can be attached when mapping to existing work

title: Update apply_fixes so it consumes only in-scope remediation for the active bead
description:
Goal:
Prevent apply_fixes from becoming a backdoor for expanding task scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- apply_fixes only receives fix-now amendments for the active bead
- planned-elsewhere and propose-new-bead findings are routed elsewhere

title: Make final_review whole-plan-aware and conservative about creating new beads
description:
Goal:
Keep final_review valuable at the end of a bead, but teach it to operate in milestone context.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- final review understands the umbrella plan and nearby bead coverage
- it can approve a bead without demanding all future work now
- it proposes a new bead only when the missing work is real and not already represented

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
Final review still matters. The change is not to remove it, but to give it the context needed to stop acting like
every bead is the entire project.

title: Add workflow tests for planned-elsewhere behavior, conservative new-bead creation, and bead approval
description:
Goal:
Prove the new review behavior with structured tests.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- tests cover all three routing cases
- quick_dev can complete a bead cleanly when later work already exists
- tests verify that new bead creation is conservative

title: Build the milestone controller for sequential bead execution
description:
Purpose:
Add the new component that selects beads and turns them into Ralph task runs.

Responsibilities:
- inspect milestone state
- inspect `.beads/`
- choose the next bead
- claim it
- create a bead-backed Ralph task
- run it through quick_dev
- reconcile the outcome back into milestone state and `.beads/`

Phase-1 scope is intentionally sequential: one active task per milestone.

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the controller can select, claim, run, and reconcile one bead at a time with explicit durable state and journal evidence
- runtime tests cover happy-path execution, interruption, blocked work, and tool failure before parallelism is considered

comments.author: planner
comments.text:
This is the new meta-project component. It should be designed so future parallel execution can grow on top of it,
but the first implementation should stay single-bead-at-a-time.

title: Define the controller state model, journal events, and stop/resume semantics
description:
Goal:
Make the controller an explicit state machine instead of a loose script.

Must define:
- idle
- selecting
- claimed
- running
- reconciling
- blocked / needs_operator
- completed

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- controller persistence is explicit
- operator can stop/resume without losing track of the active bead/task

title: Select the next bead with bv guidance and br readiness validation
description:
Goal:
Ask bv what to do next, then verify it against br's actual ready state before acting.

Selection policy:
- record the bv recommendation before claiming anything
- validate the candidate against br readiness and blocker state
- if bv suggests a blocked or missing bead, do not claim and leave an explicit operator-visible reason

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the controller can request and record a next-bead recommendation
- selection is validated against blockers and actual ready state before any claim happens
- mismatches or empty-ready situations leave milestone state in a safe blocked or needs-operator path instead of claiming the wrong bead
- deterministic tests cover single-ready, conflicting-recommendation, and no-ready cases

title: Claim a bead and create a corresponding Ralph task
description:
Goal:
Turn the selected bead into an owned task run.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- br claim/update happens explicitly
- a Ralph task/project is created from the bead-backed prompt
- milestone state records the claim and linked task id

title: Run the selected bead through the current quick_dev engine and monitor progress
description:
Goal:
Orchestrate the active task run without replacing the workflow engine.

Boundaries:
- the controller starts or resumes only the bead-linked task created in the claim step
- quick_dev remains the execution engine; this bead owns orchestration and state tracking around it
- progress and failure handling must stay inspectable through milestone state and journal events

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the controller can start or resume the bead-linked task without bypassing the current quick_dev engine
- milestone status and journal state record run start, pause, resume, success, and failure with bead and task identifiers
- workflow failure or interruption leaves an explicit controller state that can be resumed or surfaced to an operator
- integration coverage proves success, interruption-resume, and workflow-failure paths

title: Reconcile success, failure, planned-elsewhere notes, and missing-work proposals back into .beads
description:
Goal:
Close the loop from task execution back into bead-space.

Must handle:
- successful bead close
- notes/comments tied to covered-by-existing-bead findings
- mapping to existing beads
- true failure / retry states
- proposed new beads when the threshold is met

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- every run outcome produces explicit bead mutations or explicit no-mutation evidence with rationale
- milestone state, journal history, and `.beads/` mutations stay consistent enough to resume safely after any handled outcome
- reconciliation records bead ids, task ids, and mutation intent in an audit-friendly way
- downstream happy-path, planned-elsewhere, and new-bead branches can be tested independently

comments.author: planner
comments.text: Reconciliation is where this whole design either compounds or falls apart. Be explicit, durable, and conservative.

title: Handle successful bead completion and close/suggest-next reconciliation
description:
Goal:
Implement the happy path cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- completed bead is closed in br
- sync occurs
- milestone progress updates
- next-step hints can be recorded from br/bv output

title: Handle planned-elsewhere findings and existing-bead mappings during reconciliation
description:
Goal:
Persist the fact that a review concern was valid but already covered by another bead.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- mappings to existing bead ids can be recorded
- active bead can still complete without unnecessary reopen/fix loops

title: Handle propose-new-bead outcomes with conservative thresholds and dependency injection
description:
Goal:
Create a new bead only when truly necessary.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- controller checks for an existing bead first
- new bead creation includes rationale and dependency placement
- sync runs after mutation
- operator-visible evidence explains why a new bead was created

title: Create new beads parsimoniously when review uncovers genuinely missing work
description:
Goal:
Codify the policy for when new bead creation is allowed.

Policy order:
- first fix the issue now if it is in scope for the active bead
- otherwise map it to already planned work when an existing bead legitimately owns it
- only then may the controller create a new bead

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- the default choice is fix now when the finding is in scope
- covered-by-existing-bead outcomes win over new-bead creation whenever a real mapped bead exists
- any newly created bead carries rationale, dependency placement, and operator-visible evidence explaining why creation was necessary
- tests prove both no-create and create paths so the controller does not silently expand scope

title: Add sequential runtime tests for happy path, blocked path, restart, and tool failure
description:
Goal:
Validate the controller under realistic phase-1 conditions.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- tests cover success, blocked dependency, task failure, resume after interruption, and tool/adaptor failure
- sequential one-bead-at-a-time execution is proven before we consider parallelism

comments.author: planner
comments.text: Implementation requirement: controller runtime tests should use mock br/bv adapters from the shared test infrastructure (QA6) for deterministic behavior. Tests must verify not just outcomes but also the sequence of adapter calls (e.g., bv next was called before br claim, br close happened after successful review). Include tests that verify structured log output contains the expected milestone_id/bead_id/task_id fields at key decision points.

title: Deliver milestone and task CLI/UX on top of the current system
description:
Purpose:
Surface the new milestone/task model to users without forcing a disruptive rewrite underneath.

This epic owns:
- milestone commands
- task aliases/wrappers
- lineage/status output
- compatibility messaging during the transition

## Success Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- milestone commands, task aliases, lineage output, and compatibility messaging land coherently on top of the current system
- CLI tests and docs make the milestone-task mental model usable without breaking existing project commands

comments.author: planner
comments.text: A lot of confusion can be prevented just by making the CLI say the right thing consistently.

title: Add milestone create, plan, show, status, next, and run commands
description:
Goal:
Provide a first-class CLI surface for the new umbrella abstraction.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- users can create/plan/show/run milestones without dropping into internal tooling
- command output is grounded in milestone state and bead graph state

comments.author: planner
comments.text: The milestone CLI is where the new model becomes real for operators.

title: Add milestone create/plan/show/status command handlers
description:
Goal:
Implement the read-heavy command set first.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- create/plan/show/status are functional
- output reflects milestone artifacts and progress

title: Add milestone next/run commands and active milestone selection behavior
description:
Goal:
Add the execution-facing commands that talk to the controller.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
1. next shows the recommended bead.
2. run starts or resumes sequential execution.
3. Active milestone selection rules are explicit.
4. The command surface can land once controller selection and start-resume behavior exist, without waiting on later reconciliation hardening.

title: Add task aliases/wrappers around the current project commands while keeping the internal project substrate intact
description:
Goal:
Let users talk about tasks while the implementation still uses project services underneath.

Scope:
- add task aliases/wrappers for the current project commands that matter in phase 1
- keep internal storage/services on the current project substrate in phase 1
- surface the task <-> project mapping clearly in help and command output

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
1. Task commands exist or are clearly aliased.
2. Output explains the mapping where useful.
3. Backward compatibility for current project commands is preserved.

title: Show milestone and bead lineage in task/project/run output
description:
Goal:
Make normal inspection commands milestone-aware.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- show/status/history output can reveal milestone_id and bead_id
- active task/run display is useful in milestone mode

title: Keep project commands as compatibility aliases during the transition
description:
Goal:
Avoid breaking existing operators and scripts while new terminology lands.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- current project commands still work
- messaging makes it clear when project == internal task substrate

title: Update CLI reference and help text for milestone/task vocabulary
description:
Goal:
Align the docs/help output with the new mental model.

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- docs and help mention milestone/task terminology consistently
- compatibility notes explain how legacy project commands map to the new model

title: Add CLI integration tests for milestone commands, task aliases, and compatibility
description:
Goal:
Test the CLI command surface in isolation before e2e testing, catching argument parsing,
output formatting, and error handling issues early.

Test categories:

1. Milestone commands:
   - milestone create with valid/invalid inputs
   - milestone plan triggering planning flow
   - milestone show output format and content
   - milestone status with various milestone states
   - milestone next showing recommended bead
   - milestone run start/resume behavior

2. Task aliases:
   - Verify task commands dispatch to correct project commands
   - Verify output uses task terminology
   - Verify backward compatibility with project commands

3. Error handling:
   - Missing required arguments produce helpful error messages
   - Invalid milestone/bead IDs produce clear errors
   - Operations on non-existent milestones fail gracefully

4. Output formatting:
   - JSON output mode works correctly
   - Human-readable output is consistent and well-formatted
   - Progress/status output reflects actual state

## Acceptance Criteria
acceptance_criteria:
- All milestone commands tested for basic functionality
- Task aliases tested for correct dispatch
- Error messages are helpful and actionable
- Output formatting is consistent
- Tests use mock backends (from QA6) for deterministic behavior
